reason: This is a standard file permission hardening measure. The provided remediation script includes exclusions for critical filesystems like /proc and various network mounts to avoid system breakage. Changing ownership of world-writable directories to root is a common security practice and unlikely to cause lockout or critical service disruption when executed with the given precautions.
estimated_impact: filesystems affected; Potential service disruption if directories are used by applications; System stability if critical directories are modified
